title: Hydroxyl ion absorption
content: Hydroxyl ion absorption is the absorption in optical fiber s of electromagnetic wave s, including the near- infrared, due to the presence of trapped hydroxyl ions remaining from water as a contaminant. The hydroxyl (OH−) ion, can penetrate glass during or after product fabrication, resulting in significant attenuation of discrete optical wavelengths, "e.g. ", centred at 1.383 μm, used for communications via optical fibres.
